prompt:
Analyze the image and generate an open-vocabulary scene graph.
Return:
1. A short scene description.
2. Objects with normalized bounding boxes in this format: <ref>object</ref><box>[[x1, y1, x2, y2]]</box>.
3. Spatial relations in this format: <pred>predicate</pred><box>[[subject_box]]</box><box>[[object_box]]</box>.
4. Depth layers from closest to farthest.
5. Four comparative question-answer pairs about object position, depth, or size.
Use coordinates from 0 to 999. Be concise.
answer:
1. A happy close-up dog face fills the image, with its tongue out, nose centered, and blurred outdoor grass/background behind it.

2. Objects  
<ref>dog head</ref><box>[[32, 0, 999, 999]]</box>  
<ref>snout</ref><box>[[133, 219, 807, 689]]</box>  
<ref>nose</ref><box>[[382, 204, 681, 501]]</box>  
<ref>tongue</ref><box>[[188, 514, 564, 974]]</box>  
<ref>mouth</ref><box>[[213, 420, 839, 783]]</box>  
<ref>left eye</ref><box>[[295, 103, 463, 251]]</box>  
<ref>right eye</ref><box>[[761, 143, 911, 303]]</box>  
<ref>left ear</ref><box>[[184, 0, 493, 184]]</box>  
<ref>right ear</ref><box>[[850, 260, 999, 634]]</box>  
<ref>dog body</ref><box>[[681, 580, 999, 999]]</box>  
<ref>grass background</ref><box>[[0, 0, 999, 999]]</box>

3. Spatial relations  
<pred>tongue below nose</pred><box>[[188, 514, 564, 974]]</box><box>[[382, 204, 681, 501]]</box>  
<pred>nose above mouth</pred><box>[[382, 204, 681, 501]]</box><box>[[213, 420, 839, 783]]</box>  
<pred>left eye left of nose</pred><box>[[295, 103, 463, 251]]</box><box>[[382, 204, 681, 501]]</box>  
<pred>right eye right of nose</pred><box>[[761, 143, 911, 303]]</box><box>[[382, 204, 681, 501]]</box>  
<pred>dog head in front of grass background</pred><box>[[32, 0, 999, 999]]</box><box>[[0, 0, 999, 999]]</box>  
<pred>dog body behind dog head</pred><box>[[681, 580, 999, 999]]</box><box>[[32, 0, 999, 999]]</box>

4. Depth layers  
Closest: tongue, nose  
Middle: snout, mouth, eyes, ears, dog head  
Farther: dog body  
Farthest: grass background

5. Comparative Q&A  
Q: Which is lower, the nose or the tongue?  
A: The tongue is lower.

Q: Which eye is farther left in the image?  
A: The left eye is farther left.

Q: Which appears closer, the dog head or the grass background?  
A: The dog head appears closer.

Q: Which is larger, the nose or the tongue?  
A: The tongue is larger.
